title: US CPI Forecast Snapshot — July 2026 (v4.0 Quantitative Bridge Methodology)
methodology: v4.0 two-stage aggregation, exact BLS weights. ENERGY BRIDGE: AAA monthly averages — June ~$4.15/gal vs July 1-14 $3.84 (-7.5% NSA MTD, PROVISIONAL 14/31 days); full-month base ~$3.90 (-6.0%) x beta_gas 0.95 = gasoline CPI -5.7% SA. Sanity bound: contribution -0.186pp within +/-20% of (0.03266 x -6.0). Core DECOMPOSED: shelter +0.15% (BLS run-rate Apr 0.6/May 0.3/Jun 0.1 decelerating; ZORI +2.2% y/y, AptList -1.2% y/y), core goods +0.05%, core services +0.15% -> core +0.127% via /0.80557. y/y INDEX-DERIVED (Jun26 NSA ~333.85 / Jul25 NSA ~322.878). ANTI-ANCHORING: bottom-up headline ~0.0% m/m / ~3.35% y/y computed BEFORE external anchors; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% y/y; divergence 0.2pp justified component-level (market overprices back-half oil spike: July avg needs >$4.41/gal rest-of-month to flip energy positive); final moved +0.05pp toward market. June 2026 actuals (Jul 14): headline -0.4%/3.5%, core 0.0%/2.6%.
summary: JULY 2026 CPI (released Aug 12, 08:30 ET). Forecast: headline 0.0% m/m (3.4% y/y; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6%); core +0.1% m/m (2.5% y/y). Key v4.0 finding: the Hormuz oil spike is real, but MONTHLY-AVERAGE arithmetic keeps July energy a DRAG — June avg gasoline ~$4.15 vs July-to-date $3.84; retail would need to average >$4.41/gal for the rest of July to flip the energy contribution positive. The blockade premium hits the AUGUST print, not July (barring extreme escalation). Core stays soft on June's 0.0% momentum (shelter +0.1% smallest since 2021, insurance -2.0%, goods deflation). PROVISIONAL: July is 14/31 days in; the back-half oil path sets the tails. 10Y 4.57%, 2Y -14bp to 4.14%, DXY 100.92, July hike odds 17% post-CPI, Sep ~60%. FOMC Jul 28-29 lands before this release.
baseCase: Headline ~0.0% m/m / 3.4% y/y, core +0.1% / 2.5% — June's disinflation momentum persists in July because monthly-average gasoline stays below June's high average despite the blockade rebound; the oil spike is an August CPI story.

aggregation.componentSources: AAA daily national avg (Jun ~$4.15 avg, Jul 1-14 $3.84 avg, PROVISIONAL); EIA weekly retail ($3.777 Jul 6 -> $3.855 Jul 13); BLS shelter run-rate Apr/May/Jun 0.6/0.3/0.1; Zillow ZORI +2.2% y/y; Apartment List -1.2% y/y; Manheim Jun +0.1% m/m; KBB new ATP +0.4% m/m; Argus jet fuel $3.38/gal Jul 13; Insurify 2026 premium +1%; BLS NSA index May 335.123
aggregation.verification: Food(13.25)+Energy(6.193)+Core(80.557)=100.000 | Shelter(34.411)+CoreGoods(18.4)+CoreServices(27.746)=80.557 | Core identity: (0.0516+0.0092+0.0416)/0.80557=0.127% | Energy sanity bound: -0.186pp within [-0.235,-0.157] PASS | July inputs PROVISIONAL (14/31 days)

release.name: CPI
release.thesis: JULY 2026 print (Aug 12). Monthly-average arithmetic dominates: June avg gasoline ~$4.15/gal vs July-to-date $3.84 — even with the Hormuz blockade rebound (EIA $3.777 Jul 6 -> $3.855 Jul 13, WTI ~$80), July's full-month average (~$3.90 base) stays ~6% below June, keeping energy a ~-0.17pp drag. The blockade premium hits the AUGUST print unless retail averages >$4.41 for the rest of July. Core extends June's 0.0% momentum: shelter +0.15% (run-rate decelerating 0.6/0.3/0.1), goods ~flat, services +0.15% (airfare pressure vs insurance deflation) -> core +0.1% / 2.5% y/y. Bottom-up 3.35% y/y computed BEFORE anchors; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% overprice the back-half oil spike; moved +0.05pp documented. Prior = June actual (-0.4%/3.5%, core 0.0%/2.6%). PROVISIONAL — 14/31 days of July data.

scenarios.label: Base case
scenarios.outcome: Headline 0.0% to -0.1% m/m, Core +0.1% m/m — monthly-average gasoline stays below June despite the blockade rebound; core momentum from June's 0.0% persists; y/y 3.3-3.4%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Choppy, range-bound tape into FOMC (Jul 28-29): 10Y 4.50-4.60%, DXY ~100-101. A soft-but-expected print keeps Sep hike pricing ~60% — the real repricing waits for the Aug 12 confirmation. $TLT and $GLD bid on dips; $QQQ selective with rates relief; $XLE holds the blockade premium.
scenarios.invalidation: Retail gasoline averages >$4.41/gal over the rest of July (flips energy positive), or core re-accelerates to +0.3% on airfares/tariffs.

scenarios.label: Hotter inflation surprise
scenarios.outcome: Headline +0.2% to +0.4% m/m, Core +0.2-0.3% m/m — extreme escalation: blockade sustains AND retail gasoline spikes >$4.40 back-half; airfares jump on jet fuel ($3.38/gal and rising); tariff passthrough lifts goods; y/y back toward 3.7%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y spikes 4.70%+, 2Y reverses, DXY 101.5+, Sep hike >80% and a second 2026 hike prices in. Equities extend the selloff — $SPY PUT / $VIX CALL setups. $XLE and $GLD squeeze on the energy/geopolitical tail.
scenarios.invalidation: Truce re-forms and WTI collapses below $70, or the hot print is a one-off airfare spike while goods keep deflating.

scenarios.label: Cooler disinflation impulse
scenarios.outcome: Headline -0.3% to -0.4% m/m, Core 0.0% m/m — Hormuz de-escalation/truce re-forms and oil gives back the spike; June's core momentum repeats (shelter +0.1%, insurance deflation, goods soft); y/y dips toward 3.2%.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: 10Y drops below 4.45%, 2Y extends the -14bp move, DXY <100. Sep hike odds fall under 40%, late-2026 cut pricing revives. Risk-on: $QQQ +1-2%, $TLT strong, $IWM and homebuilders rally, $UAL bounces on cheaper jet fuel.
scenarios.invalidation: The blockade escalation dominates the back half of July regardless, or the market already priced the cool outcome.

setups.bias: Long duration — cool July path on monthly-average math
setups.trigger: Core stays <=+0.1% and retail gasoline avg stays <$4.00; 10Y fails 4.60%
setups.invalidation: 10Y breaks 4.70% on extreme escalation or hot core (airfares/tariffs).

setups.trigger: DXY below 101 and real yields compress; blockade persists without full escalation
setups.invalidation: DXY above 101.5 with 10Y above 4.75% kills the haven bid.

setups.bias: Cautiously long — rates relief on soft July trajectory
setups.trigger: Core momentum <=+0.1% holds and FOMC (Jul 28-29) stays on hold
setups.invalidation: Hot core or 10Y >4.70%; chip-capex headline risk (IBM -25% precedent).

setups.bias: Tactical long while the blockade premium holds (XLE +3.4% today)
setups.trigger: WTI holds above $78 and Hormuz transits stay ~34/day vs 88 baseline
setups.invalidation: De-escalation sends WTI below $70 — the premium unwinds fast.

setups.bias: Avoid now; flip long on de-escalation — jet fuel $3.38/gal and rising
setups.trigger: Entry only if WTI breaks below $72 with bookings intact
setups.invalidation: Sustained blockade pushes jet fuel back toward April's $4.88 peak.

setups.bias: Protective only on the escalation tail — VIX ~15.5-17, premium not cheap
setups.trigger: VIX breakout above 20 on blockade widening or hot core repricing
setups.invalidation: Cool July trajectory + FOMC hold crushes vol back below 14.

watchItems.label: MEASURED MONTH
watchItems.note: Snapshot measures JULY price changes. Prior = June actual (Jul 14): headline -0.4% m/m / +3.5% y/y, core 0.0% / +2.6% (energy -5.7%, gasoline -9.7%, shelter +1% -> 0.1%).

watchItems.label: AAA Gasoline Bridge (v4.0)
watchItems.value: Jun avg ~$4.15 -> Jul 1-14 avg $3.84 (-7.5% NSA MTD)
watchItems.note: PROVISIONAL. Full-month base ~$3.90 (-6.0%) x beta 0.95 = gasoline CPI -5.7% SA, contribution -0.186pp (sanity bound PASS). Flip-positive threshold: rest-of-July avg >$4.41/gal. EIA weekly: $3.777 (Jul 6) -> $3.855 (Jul 13) — rebound in progress.

watchItems.label: Hormuz / Iran (JULY BACK-HALF RISK)
watchItems.note: WTI ~$79.8 (+2.1%), Brent ~$85 (+9.6% Monday = biggest since 2020). 2 UAE tankers hit, CENTCOM strikes 3rd night. Trump dropped the 20% transit fee for Gulf investment deals — capped oil intraday. Path-dependent: sustained blockade = hot tail; truce = cool tail.

watchItems.label: Anti-Anchoring Log (v4.0)
watchItems.note: External: prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% y/y (>3.5 68c, >3.6 42c, >3.7 21c, repriced DOWN post-June). Divergence 0.2pp > 0.15pp threshold — justified: market overprices back-half oil spike vs monthly-average arithmetic. Final moved +0.05pp to 3.4%. Cleveland Fed July nowcast not yet published — recheck next run.

watchItems.label: Core Momentum
watchItems.note: Shelter +0.1% (smallest since Jan 2021; run-rate 0.6/0.3/0.1 decelerating; ZORI +2.2% y/y, AptList -1.2% y/y). Motor vehicle insurance -2.0% (2nd straight big drop). Goods broadly deflating. Upside: airfares (jet fuel re-spike) + tariff passthrough (nonfuel import +0.8% m/m in May).

watchItems.label: Fed Path / FOMC
watchItems.note: Post-June-CPI repricing: July effectively off; 2Y -14bp to 4.14% (biggest drop since Feb). Warsh testimony Day 2 on Jul 15. FOMC Jul 28-29 lands BEFORE this print (Aug 12) — July CPI shapes September.

watchItems.label: 10Y UST / DXY / VIX / SPX
watchItems.note: Jul 14 close: SPX +0.4%, Nasdaq +0.9%, banks strong (GS +7-9%, JPM record), IBM -25% drag on Dow. 10Y regime line: <4.45% growth relief, >4.70% risk-off. VIX today unverified (~15.5-17).

keyDrivers: MONTHLY-AVERAGE ARITHMETIC (the v4.0 correction): June avg gasoline ~$4.15/gal vs July-to-date $3.84 — despite the Hormuz blockade rebound, July's full-month average (~$3.90 base) stays ~6% below June. Energy remains a ~-0.17pp DRAG in July; the oil spike hits the AUGUST print unless retail averages >$4.41/gal rest-of-month.

June core momentum: core 0.0% m/m (weakest since May 2020), shelter +0.1% (smallest since 2021), insurance -2.0%, broad goods deflation. July core forecast +0.1% on persistence, capped by airfare/tariff upside.
Hormuz is a BACK-HALF path risk, not a level story: blockade resumed Jul 14 4pm ET; WTI ~$80; retail pass-through runs 1-3 weeks — decides whether July lands base or hot, and loads August either way.
Anti-anchoring: bottom-up 3.35% y/y computed before external anchors; prediction markets ~3.5-3.6% overprice the back-half spike; documented +0.05pp move only.
Fed: July hike odds collapsed to 17% post-CPI; September ~60% — this print (Aug 12) lands directly in the September pricing window.
Strong USD (DXY 100.92, off 101.80 high) keeps core-goods import deflation working against tariff passthrough (nonfuel imports +0.8% m/m in May; June data Jul 15).
PROVISIONAL / capped confidence 55: July is 14/31 days in, vol regime active (gasoline monthly move >5%, supply shock) — scenario span 0.8pp, no tail below 20%.
risks: Extreme escalation (top risk): blockade widens or strikes hit Gulf infrastructure — retail gasoline >$4.40 back-half flips July energy positive and invalidates the cool/base call.
Core re-acceleration: airfares on the jet-fuel spike ($3.38/gal, +20.7% y/y fares in May) plus tariff passthrough could push core to +0.2-0.3%.
PROVISIONAL data risk: 14/31 days; the back-half oil path swings the energy component ~0.2pp either way.
Consensus convergence: post-June repricing already moved markets down (Sep hike 90%->60%); a merely-soft July print may be 'priced' by Aug 12, dulling the rally.
PPI pipeline: July PPI (~Aug 13) and June import prices (Jul 15) test the goods-deflation assumption.
Warsh testimony Day 2 (Jul 15): a hawkish surprise re-prices the front end before the data even lands.
